title: Policy Development and Review Guide
bodyHtml:
I. Purpose

This guide provides Al-Ittihad Private School (IPS) with a consistent process for developing, approving, implementing, monitoring and reviewing policies. It is intended to help IPS policy owners prepare policies that are clear, compliant, practical, measurable and aligned with the school’s strategic priorities, operational requirements and regulatory obligations.

II. Scope

This guide applies to the development and review of IPS policies that affect students, staff, parents, school operations, compliance, teaching and learning, assessment, wellbeing, safeguarding, administration, technology, finance, facilities or any other area requiring a formal and consistent position. It applies to new policies, major reviews, minor amendments, procedures and related guidance documents.

III. Guiding principles

PrincipleMeaning for IPS
AlignmentPolicies should align with IPS vision, strategic priorities, values and regulatory requirements.
ClarityPolicies should be written in clear language so staff, students and parents can understand expectations.
AccountabilityEach policy should have a clearly identified owner responsible for implementation, monitoring and review.
ConsultationRelevant stakeholders should be consulted before a policy is approved.
PracticalityPolicies should be realistic, implementable and supported by procedures or guidance where required.
ReviewPolicies should be monitored and reviewed to ensure they remain current and fit for purpose.

V. Policy hierarchy

IPS maintains a simple document hierarchy — Policy, Procedure, Guideline, and Form or template — so that staff understand the difference between an approved position, how it is carried out, supporting advice, and the records that capture evidence.
